overview: Green tribal beatdown focused on overwhelming the board with trampling Bear tokens and applying relentless pressure. Wins by out-scaling opponents through token generation, direct damage, and unblockable threats that punish reactive strategies.
win_conditions: Ayula attacking with multiple tokens, often boosted by pump spells.
Unblocked trampling damage is the primary win condition.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 Forests and a turn-1 or turn-2 threat (Ayula, Werebear, or a cheap bear generator/pump).
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple high-cost spells without early threats, or hands that cannot cast Ayula/Werebear by turn 2-3.

early_game: Turn 2: Cast Werebear or Ayula if mana allows
otherwise, play a land and hold for turn 3.
Sequence lands to hit 3 mana on turn 2 or 4 mana on turn 3.
Attack immediately to establish board presence and trigger token generation.
mid_game: avoid overcommitting to one side of the board if you cannot follow up.
Use card draw to maintain hand size, as bear tribal decks often run thin.
Protect key threats with pump spells or protection if available.
Commit threats when you have removal or protection ready for the opponent's answer.
Hold back if the opponent has a board wipe or hard counter in play.
Use tokens to trade efficiently and force unfavorable blocks.
late_game: Ayula attacking with multiple tokens, often boosted by pump spells.
Unblocked trampling damage is the primary win condition.
Go all-in when you have established a board of 3+ creatures, the opponent has no sweepers, or you can clear their blockers with removal/pump.
Also commit when you have card advantage and can out-value their removal.

sequencing_tips: Ayula's token generation scales with combat, so attacking with multiple creatures maximizes value.
Watch out for cantrips or cheap removal that can trade favorably against your tokens.
Avoid overcommitting to a single attack if you suspect a board wipe
use your tokens to probe for answers.

matchups.advice: Use cheap threats, multikicker, or split the cast. Attack with multiple small creatures to force counter decisions. Use pump spells on resolution or cast threats in response to counterspells.
Apply early pressure to race them before they hit their big mana. Use removal to answer their ramp targets or finishers. Consider hate bears or discard if the format allows.
Stabilize with removal and board wipes. Play defensively until you can establish your own board, then transition to offense. Use life gain to survive early damage.

Pressure consistently to force them to use resources on interaction rather than combo/finishers. Use unblockable threats and pump spells to bypass board wipes. Out-value them with card draw and efficient removal.
Win by out-resourcing with better card draw, more efficient removal, or superior pump spells. Trade creatures efficiently and prioritize clearing their Ayula/Werebear first.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes, targeted removal, and counterspells.
Note if the opponent is playing sweepers or hate cards.
Track their mana development to anticipate when they can cast their answers.
